ksa_text: Skill in writing shell scripts to automate data operations, including file manipulation, job scheduling, environment configuration, log processing, and pipeline orchestration on Linux systems.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of basic system administration, network, and operating system hardening techniques.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of basic system administration, network, and operating system hardening techniques.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although Technical Foundations is an edge domain for the VP AI/ML, this skill KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.463) and conceptual alignment to include at the vp level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Technical Foundations), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.463). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the VP AI/ML versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Technical foundation knowledge is validated by DCWF, NICE framework, and engineering director job postings as required for technical leadership oversight.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: while the domain is relevant, this specific skill KSA is better attributed to a more specialized adjacent role. Revised to exclude.

decision_rationale: Initially included but overturned through adversarial review. Technical Foundations skill KSA does not meet the quality or scope threshold for the VP AI/ML after cross-framework validation.
